aliases: how to use persistent storage in Kubernetes
Kubernetes volume access modes RWO RWX
how to expand a PVC in Kubernetes

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: Kubernetes 1.31: PV deletion protection finalizers GA (2024-08); Kubernetes 1.29: ReadWriteOncePod GA (2023-12)

constraints: A PVC can bind to exactly ONE PV -- the binding is one-to-one and exclusive until the PVC is deleted
Volume expansion requires allowVolumeExpansion: true on the StorageClass -- PVCs using classes without this flag CANNOT be resized
Recycle reclaim policy is DEPRECATED since Kubernetes 1.22 -- use dynamic provisioning with Delete or Retain instead
VolumeSnapshot requires the CSI driver to implement snapshot capability AND the snapshot-controller + CRDs to be installed cluster-wide
NEVER delete a PVC while a Pod is actively writing to the volume -- Storage Object in Use Protection defers deletion but data corruption can still occur during ungraceful termination

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Running a StatefulSet with per-replica persistent storage and need help with headless services, ordering, and operators
Using ephemeral storage (emptyDir, configMap, secret) that does not persist beyond Pod lifetime
Deploying stateless applications without persistent data

inputs_needed.question: What storage backend or cloud provider are you using?
How many nodes/pods need simultaneous access to the volume?
Do you want volumes created automatically or pre-provisioned by an admin?
